km_name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Climate, Carbon, Energy and Buildings
purpose: Binary Yes/No SpecificTopic decisions for the climate_energy family. This simplified KM owns its tag-level reconciliation and emits only final Yes tags for this family.

rules: Answer every tag in this KM independently as Yes or No before producing the family final list.
Emit only tags answered Yes in final_specific_topics_from_this_family.
Do not emit candidates, centrality labels, or nearby labels as final tags.
If two tags overlap, keep both only when the source has distinct operative evidence for both; otherwise answer Yes only for the more directly satisfied decision question.
Reject incidental, background, implementation-actor-only, and keyword-only evidence even when a semantic signal appears.
The standalone SpecificTopic reconciler has been removed; this family KM is responsible for suppressing weak/adjacent labels before code consolidation.
weak_evidence_policy: Weak or not-observed tags remain in the enum, but need direct source evidence at runtime.
Do not use weak master examples as independent proof; use them only as cautionary boundary tests.

tag_decisions.tag: canonical SpecificTopic value from this KM
tag_decisions.evidence_excerpt: required when answer is Yes; empty string allowed when No
tag_decisions.nearby_label_if_any: optional canonical tag that better fits the evidence

final_specific_topics_from_this_family: all tags from tag_decisions with answer=Yes, already reconciled by this family KM

tags.definition: Performance requirements for buildings, commonly energy, emissions, environmental, or operational performance standards.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Building Performance Standards"?
yes_when: The source sets building-level performance requirements, ratings, thresholds, or compliance duties.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The text concerns construction products, land-use planning, or energy equipment without building-level performance duties.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "to promote the disclosure of information about the energy efficiency of buildings". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Building Performance Standards"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source sets building-level performance requirements, ratings, thresholds, or compliance duties.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "building performance" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source sets building-level performance requirements, ratings, thresholds, or compliance duties.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Building Performance Standards"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The text concerns construction products, land-use planning, or energy equipment without building-level performance duties.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Building Performance Standards"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

tags.definition: Measures to reduce, avoid, price, limit, or remove greenhouse gas emissions or accelerate the low-carbon transition.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Climate Change Mitigation"?
yes_when: The source centrally targets greenhouse gas reductions, decarbonisation, net-zero, climate targets, carbon markets/pricing, renewable-energy expansion, energy efficiency, or mitigation planning.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: Do not tag merely because a pollution, waste, transport, or environmental law has indirect climate benefits. Require an explicit climate, greenhouse gas, carbon, renewable-energy, or energy-efficiency mechanism.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Climate Change Mitigation"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source centrally targets greenhouse gas reductions, decarbonisation, net-zero, climate targets, carbon markets/pricing, renewable-energy expansion, energy efficiency, or mitigation planning.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "climate mitigation" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source centrally targets greenhouse gas reductions, decarbonisation, net-zero, climate targets, carbon markets/pricing, renewable-energy expansion, energy efficiency, or mitigation planning.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Climate Change Mitigation"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "Do not tag merely because a pollution, waste, transport, or environmental law has indirect climate benefits. Require an explicit climate, greenhouse gas, carbon, renewable-energy, or energy-efficiency mechanism.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Climate Change Mitigation"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "imposes obligations on Australia to take action to reduce greenhouse gas emissions;". Standing alone, should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Climate Change Mitigation"?
test_case.reasoning_check: The master reason was classified as weak/cautionary; the LLM must look for central legal duties, not rely on weak contextual association.

There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.

Evidence-packet support for this tag is weak, indirect, or absent; answer Yes only when the current source text independently and directly satisfies the definition, decision question, and a yes_when rule.
no_when: The source sets product energy labels or building energy performance without industrial audit duties.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.
Do not answer Yes from analogy to weak master examples, broad ESG relatedness, a title-only hunch, or the absence of a better label.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "In het kader van het beleid op het gebied van energiebesparing verzamelt, analyseert en bewerkt Onze Minister inlichtingen en gegevens met betrekking tot: a. energieverbruik; b. maatregelen ter verbetering van de energie-efficiëntie;". test_case.reasoning_check: The master reason was classified as weak/cautionary; the LLM must look for central legal duties, not rely on weak contextual association.
